title: DAPR Sidecar Injection Fails to Inject
body:
## In what area(s)? > /area runtime > /area operator > /area placement > /area docs > /area test-and-release /area injector > /area scheduler > /area sentry ## What version of Dapr? 1.16.2 > 1.1.x
1.0.x
edge: output of `git describe --dirty` ## Expected Behavior When pods are annotated for injection, they should be injected with DAPR sidecar. ## Actual Behavior In an anecdotally small percentage of overall injection, pods that are annotated for injection are not getting injected. I've enabled watchdog to restart/kill (not sure which it does) these pods and that has been successful for now. This is related to [#4270]() but in my case I am not using Weave Net. ## Steps to Reproduce the Problem I don't have steps to reproduce currently, however I have experienced this both in AKS with the extension and in GKE with the helm install. Are there meaningful logs in the sidecar-injector? Or perhaps if this is a known issue and the cause is known someone could point me in the right direction to contribute to the operator. ## Release Note RELEASE NOTE:

comments.body: Hey @sethtomy this happens occasionally but the fix is to enable the Dapr operator watchdog: https://docs.dapr.io/concepts/dapr-services/operator/#injector-watchdog

@alicejgibbons thanks for your response!

I'm currently using the watchdog and it does work well enough. 

My concern is the existence of watchdog is perhaps a bandaid. Is it an issue in the sidecar injector or the K8s API/eventing?

Is there an issue or otherwise as to why this is happening to fix the root cause or can anyone point me in a direction?

It is indeed a bandaid. Is it happening on initial startup or on cluster events like upgrades/downgrades? The only time I have seen it happen is when the networking setup is something exotic or there is other mutating webhooks competing. Are you using a service mesh or something else that might be involved here?

The relevant logs are as follows:
```
time="2026-01-23T18:05:11.262255551Z" level=error msg="Admission succeeded, but pod was not patched. No sidecar injected for ..." 
```

Next time it happens it would be great to see the log output and then go from there. You can also enable debug logs for the sidecar injector.
If I had to guess I fit under the network side. Not using a service mesh. This log is incredibly helpful thank you. I'll keep a look out and report back if I can help.

This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has not had activity in the last 90 days. It will be closed in the next 7 days unless it is tagged (pinned, good first issue, help wanted or triaged/resolved) or other activity occurs. Thank you for your contributions.

I'm also experiencing a similar issue to this and #4270. The easiest way to reproduce it is with `k3d` where you just install the latest helm version. Then do a `service docker stop` and `service docker start` and you'll reproduce the issue 9 times out of 10 (if not 10/10).  

I can also confirm that setting `dapr_operator.watchInterval: 15s` fixes it. :)
